# pranithjain.qzz.io - full catalog

> LLM-ingestible catalog of every tool, surface, and AI-agent endpoint on pranithjain.qzz.io. 
> This is a single concatenated document - every section is self-contained. The site has a live 
> DFIR + Threat Intel platform and an MCP server; the live endpoints are noted in their sections.

## Overview

Pranith Jain is a security analyst who builds and runs a live, edge-deployed DFIR and 
threat-intelligence platform at https://pranithjain.qzz.io . The platform has three surfaces:

- A personal portfolio (/, /about, /skills, /experience, /projects).
- A 130+ tool DFIR toolkit (/dfir/*) covering IOC checks, phishing analysis, CVE triage, 
  Sigma/KQL/SPL/YARA rule conversion, email defense, IAM analysis, and more.
- A 124+ surface live threat-intelligence platform (/threatintel/*) with ransomware tracking, 
  IOC correlation, CVE/KEV feeds, actor timelines, breach monitoring, daily briefings, and an 
  autonomous case-study blog.

The platform is open at the edge - no signup, no API key required for browsing. Sensitive 
endpoints (admin, write operations) require an API key or admin token issued at /api/v1/admin/keys.

## DFIR toolkit tools (130+ routes)

- [DFIR Catalog](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/catalog) (dfir): Every DFIR tool, searchable, grouped by category.
- [Toolkit vs VirusTotal / ANY.RUN / Hybrid Analysis / URLScan](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/vs) (dfir): Side-by-side comparison of the DFIR toolkit with hosted sandboxes and URL scanners.
- [IOC Investigator](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/ioc-investigate) (dfir): Cross-source investigation hub — paste any indicator type and pivot across all sources.
- [IOC Extractor](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/extract) (dfir): Pull IOCs from any text blob — refang-aware.
- [IOC Lifecycle](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/ioc-lifecycle) (dfir): Track an IOC from collection to enrichment to retirement.
- [Certificate Transparency Monitor](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/ct-monitor) (dfir): Watch CT logs for new certificates matching your watchlist.
- [Abuse Reputation](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/abuse-rep) (dfir): Cross-source reputation: AbuseIPDB, Spamhaus, OTX, URLhaus.
- [X-VERDIKT Multi-Source Verdict](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/x-verdikt) (dfir): Streaming verdicts from X (Twitter) intelligence feeds.
- [Malware Analyzer](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/malware-analyzer) (dfir): PE / ELF / Mach-O static analysis with import hashing + section entropy.
- [Infostealer Log Parser](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/stealer-parser) (dfir): Parse RedLine / Raccoon / Vidar / LummaC stealer logs — credentials, system, browser data.
- [Bloom Filter Lookup](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/bloom) (dfir): Membership-test against a corpus of known-bad indicators.
- [Decoder](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/decode) (dfir): base64 — hex — url — rot13 — zlib — gzip — chained auto-detection.
- [Encoder](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/encoder) (dfir): Reverse of Decoder — encode any text to any of the supported formats.
- [Hash Calculator](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/hash-calc) (dfir): MD5 — SHA1 — SHA256 — SHA512 — SSDEEP — TLSH — drag a file in.
- [Timestamp Converter](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/timestamp) (dfir): Epoch — Windows FILETIME — Unix — human — bidirectional.
- [PE Static Analyzer Lite](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/pe) (dfir): Sections, imports, exports, version info — 0x12 lite profile.
- [APK Analyzer](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/apk-analyzer) (dfir): Manifest + permissions + signing certs + native libs.
- [EXIF / Metadata Parser](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/exif) (dfir): EXIF — IPTC — XMP — MakerNotes — camera, GPS, software fingerprints.
- [Plist & Protobuf Decoder](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/plist-protobuf) (dfir): Apple binary plist + protobuf human-readable view.
- [Punycode / Homoglyph Viewer](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/punycode) (dfir): Visualise IDN homograph attacks — Cyrillic vs Latin lookalikes.
- [PowerShell Deobfuscator](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/powershell-deobf) (dfir): Unroll encoded / base64 / invoke-expression chains — step by step.
- [PCAP Triage](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/pcap-triage) (dfir): Protocol breakdown — top talkers — DNS / HTTP / TLS summaries.
- [Registry Hive Explorer](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/registry-hive) (dfir): Browse — search — diff Windows registry hives offline.
- [EVTX Parser Lite](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/evtx) (dfir): Parse Windows Event Log files — event IDs, channels, time-range filter.
- [SQLite Artifact Explorer](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/sqlite) (dfir): Browser profile — chat history — mobile backups — query in-browser via WASM.
- [iOS Backup Explorer](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/ios-backup) (dfir): Manifest.db — plists — SQLite artifacts from a local iTunes backup.
- [Web Server Log Analyzer](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/web-log) (dfir): Apache — nginx — IIS access logs — anomaly detection + pivots.
- [Prefetch Analyzer Lite](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/prefetch) (dfir): Parse Windows Prefetch files — execution evidence, run count, last run time.
- [REGSCOPE Registry Analyzer](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/regscope) (dfir): Multi-hive registry scope: persistence, autoruns, services, scheduled tasks.
- [Domain Investigator](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/domain-investigator) (dfir): Cross-source domain investigation hub — 6 aliases route here (domain-rep, webcheck, etc.).
- [WHOIS History Explorer](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/whois-history) (dfir): Historical WHOIS pivots — registrant, nameserver, status changes.
- [ASN Lookup](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/asn) (dfir): ASN details — prefix ranges — peer relationships.
- [Certificate Search](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/cert-search) (dfir): crt.sh-style CT log search for a domain — subdomains — cert chain.
- [Subdomain Takeover](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/takeover) (dfir): Detect dangling DNS records vulnerable to subdomain takeover.
- [DNSCOPE Infrastructure Map](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/dnscope) (dfir): 
- [Host Graph](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/host-graph) (dfir): Graph of related domains, IPs, and ASNs for a target.
- [Wayback Machine](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/wayback) (dfir): Search historical snapshots for a URL — changes over time.
- [IP Geolocation](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/ip-geo) (dfir): IP — country / city / ASN / org / hosting type.
- [Passive DNS](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/passive-dns) (dfir): Historical DNS resolution data for infrastructure tracking — migrations + fast-flux detection.
- [Asset Intelligence](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/asset-intel) (dfir): Aggregate asset inventory — domains, subdomains, services, certificates.
- [Exposed Host](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/exposed-host) (dfir): Per-host exposure score and evidence — services, versions, CVEs.
- [Open Directory Scanner](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/open-directory) (dfir): Detect misconfigured web servers exposing file listings.
- [URL Preview](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/url-preview) (dfir): Safe, sandboxed preview of a URL — headers, redirects, screenshot.
- [Email Defense](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/email-defense) (dfir): SPF / DKIM / DMARC / BIMI audit with failure modes called out.
- [Phishing Analyzer](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/phishing) (dfir): URL + sender + header analysis with risk score.
- [DMARC Analyzer](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/dmarc-analyzer) (dfir): Parse a DMARC aggregate report (RUA) — alignment, volume, failures.
- [EML Extractor](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/eml) (dfir): Headers — body — attachments — URL / hash extraction from a .eml file.
- [Email Deliverability Tester](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/email-deliverability) (dfir): Paste or upload a raw .eml to get spam score, SPF/DKIM/DMARC alignment, and inbox-placement suggestions.
- [Email Reputation](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/email-rep) (dfir): Sender domain + IP reputation with deliverability signals.
- [Email OSINT Profile](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/email-osnit) (dfir): Build digital identity from email — GitHub, Gravatar, breach, reputation, DNS, PGP.
- [PhishBook](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/phishbook) (dfir): Curated playbook of phishing patterns, lures, and IOCs.
- [PHISHOPS](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/phishops) (dfir): Phishing-as-a-service operator catalog and tracking.
- [URL Reputation](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/url-rep) (dfir): Cross-source URL reputation — PhishTank, OpenPhish, Google Safe Browsing.
- [Username Investigator (alias)](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/username-investigator) (dfir): Alias of /dfir/username — the canonical page.
- [Phone OSINT](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/phone-osint) (dfir): Phone number — carrier, country, line type, breach presence.
- [Weather OSINT](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/weather-osint) (dfir): Reverse geocoding + historical weather for a timestamp + coordinates.
- [SOCMINT](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/socmint) (dfir): Social-media intelligence — X / Reddit / Telegram / Mastodon pivots.
- [OSINT Mapper](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/osint-mapper) (dfir): Build a mind-map of an investigation — nodes are entities, edges are pivots.
- [Breach Lookup](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/breach) (dfir): Email / username / domain — cross-correlate public breach corpora.
- [Reverse Image Search](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/reverse-image) (dfir): Multi-engine reverse image — Google, Yandex, TinEye, Bing.
- [Brand Impersonation](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/brand-impersonation) (dfir): Detect typosquats / look-alike domains targeting your brand.
- [Image Fingerprint](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/image-fingerprint) (dfir): Perceptual hash (pHash, dHash) for image clustering & de-duplication.
- [Screenshot Intel](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/screenshot-intel) (dfir): Extract text + URLs + indicators from a screenshot — OCR pipeline.
- [CVE Lookup](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/cve) (dfir): Single-CVE detail — NVD, KEV, EPSS, exploit availability.
- [CVE Prioritizer](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/cve-prioritizer) (dfir): CVSS + EPSS + KEV + ransomware-use — single patch-priority call.
- [CVE Resources Catalog](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/vuln-toolkit) (dfir): Curated list of CVE databases, exploit trackers, vendor PSIRTs.
- [OSV Dependency Scan](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/osv-scan) (dfir): Paste a manifest.json / package-lock / requirements.txt — known vulns.
- [Rule Converter](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/rule-converter) (dfir): Sigma — KQL — SPL — YARA via one canonical IR.
- [YARA Workbench](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/yara-workbench) (dfir): Collaborative YARA editor with malware test corpus.
- [Threat Graph](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/threat-graph) (dfir): Indicator — relationship graph — visual pivot from any node.
- [ATTMAP-AI](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/attmap-ai) (dfir): AI-assisted mapping of detection rules to ATT&CK techniques.
- [Hunting Query Generator](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/hunting-query-generator) (dfir): AI-assisted KQL / SPL / Lucene generation from a hypothesis.
- [AI Rule Generator](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/ai-rule-generator) (dfir): Generate a Sigma/YARA rule from a natural-language description.
- [FP Lens](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/fp-lens) (dfir): False-positive analyst — score a detection against historical FPs.
- [IR Playbooks](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/ir-playbooks) (dfir): Step-by-step playbooks for common incident types.
- [Tools About](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/tools/about) (dfir): About the DFIR toolkit — principles, design, and feature flags.
- [TRACERULES](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/tracerules) (dfir): Trace a rule back to its source intel — coverage and lineage.
- [STIX Workbench](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/stix-workbench) (dfir): Build, view, and export STIX 2.x bundles (canonical entry — /dfir/stix, /dfir/stix-builder, /dfir/taxii, /dfir/report-ingest all redirect here).
- [AWS IAM Analyzer](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/iam-analyzer) (dfir): Parse a downloaded IAM policy — find privilege escalation paths.
- [GCP IAM Analyzer](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/gcp-iam) (dfir): GCP IAM policy + role analyzer.
- [Azure RBAC Analyzer](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/azure-rbac) (dfir): Azure RBAC role assignments — least-privilege check.
- [Security Group Analyzer](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/sg-analyzer) (dfir): AWS security group visualizer — 0.0.0.0/0 + port exposure heatmap.
- [CloudTrail Triage](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/cloudtrail-triage) (dfir): Filter CloudTrail logs for an incident timeframe — IAM, EC2, S3, KMS.
- [K8s RBAC Analyzer](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/k8s-rbac) (dfir): Kubernetes Role/ClusterRole analyzer — risky verbs, secrets access.
- [Terraform Scanner](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/terraform-scan) (dfir): Static analysis of HCL — misconfigurations + drift.
- [Non-Human Identity (NHI)](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/nhi) (dfir): Catalogue service accounts, API keys, OAuth grants.
- [Zero-Trust AI Agents](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/zero-trust-ai-agents) (dfir): Verify identity + intent for autonomous agent actions.
- [Prompt Injection](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/prompt-injection) (dfir): Test a prompt against a curated set of injection payloads.
- [MCP Audit](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/mcp-audit) (dfir): Audit a Model Context Protocol server for tool-poisoning vectors.
- [Agent Investigator](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/agent) (dfir): Investigate an autonomous agent — tool calls, prompt history, exfil.
- [Agent Map](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/agent-map) (dfir): 
- [INSIGHT-AI](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/insight-ai) (dfir): AI-assisted incident summarisation and pattern detection.
- [QUERYCRAFT-AI](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/querycraft-ai) (dfir): AI-assisted KQL / SPL / Lucene generation.
- [CHRONO-AI](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/chrono-ai) (dfir): AI-assisted timeline reconstruction from logs + reports.
- [MALBRIEF-AI](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/malbrief-ai) (dfir): AI-assisted malware family briefing from sample + sandbox output.
- [VERDIKT-AI](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/verdikt-ai) (dfir): AI-assisted IOC verdict — explain cross-source disagreement.
- [OpenAPI Auditor](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/openapi-audit) (dfir): Lint an OpenAPI spec — missing auth, schema issues, PII exposure.
- [GraphQL Auditor](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/graphql-audit) (dfir): Introspection + query depth/complexity + authz analysis.
- [JWT Inspector](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/jwt) (dfir): Decode — verify — alg-confusion check — claim analysis.
- [Security Headers Analyzer](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/sec-headers) (dfir): CORS — CSP — HSTS — X-Frame-Options — graded report.
- [Live Security Headers](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/sec-headers-live) (dfir): Third-party live HSTS/CSP/X-Frame-Options scan via IntoDNS.ai with ready-to-paste Nginx/Apache/Caddy/Cloudflare configs.
- [Secret Scanner](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/secret-scan) (dfir): Scan a text blob / repo for API keys, tokens, private keys.
- [Google Dorks Builder](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/google-dorks) (dfir): Compose a Google dork for a target — site:, inurl:, filetype:.
- [Log Parser](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/log-parser) (dfir): Generic log parser — pattern detection + anomaly highlighting.
- [DFIR Copilot](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/copilot) (dfir): Conversational copilot — ask in plain English, get a runbook.
- [Multi-Search](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/multi-search) (dfir): Query 30+ intel sources in parallel — paste an IOC or entity.
- [TRACER](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/tracer) (dfir): Cross-chain transaction tracer for AML and ransomware investigations.
- [TRACEPULSE](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/tracepulse) (dfir): Real-time crypto flow monitor — alerts on suspicious wallet activity.
- [QUICKTRACE](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/quicktrace) (dfir): Quick lookup for a crypto address or transaction hash.
- [PIVEX](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/pivex) (dfir): Pivot explorer — graph-style pivots from any entity.
- [Report Analyzer](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/report-analyzer) (dfir): AI summary — IOC extraction — MITRE TTP mapping — STIX bundle.
- [Report Composer](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/report-composer) (dfir): Cover — summary — findings — IOCs — sources — TLP — export to PDF/DOCX.
- [Export Hub](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/export-hub) (dfir): Export IOCs to STIX 2.1, MISP, Sigma, YARA, Snort, Suricata, CSV.
- [Blocklist Export](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/blocklists) (dfir): Generate network blocklists (pfSense, MikroTik, Cisco) from IOCs.
- [Investigation Notebooks](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/notebooks) (dfir): Persistent notes, IOC snapshots, and findings for DFIR investigations.
- [PGP Tool](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/pgp-tool) (dfir): Generate — encrypt — sign — verify PGP messages in-browser.
- [Tor Gateway](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/tor-gateway) (dfir): Browser-routed Tor gateway for safe .onion lookups.
- [Privacy Toolkit](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/privacy) (dfir): Privacy hygiene: tracker audit, browser fingerprint, cookie analysis.
- [GRC Toolkit](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/grc) (dfir): Control mapping — risk register — vendor assessment.
- [LOLBins](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/lolbins) (dfir): Living-off-the-land binaries — search by binary or behaviour.
- [Data Classification](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/data-classification) (dfir): Tag data with sensitivity + handling requirements.
- [Privacy Hub](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/privacy-hub) (dfir): GDPR / CCPA references, DPIA templates, privacy notice generator.
- [Personal Security](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/personal-security) (dfir): OPSEC checklist — threat-modelling for individuals.
- [DLP Scan](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/dlp-scan) (dfir): Data-loss-prevention scan for files + clipboard + screenshots.
- [Linux IR Triage](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/linux-triage) (dfir): Bash one-liners for live Linux incident response.
- [ATT&CK Navigator](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/attack-navigator) (dfir): Layered ATT&CK matrix — coverage heatmap, gap analysis.
- [Attack Chain](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/attack-chain) (dfir): Visualise a multi-stage attack as a connected kill-chain.
- [Cyber Kill Chain](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/kill-chain) (dfir): Lockheed Martin 7-phase kill chain with ATT&CK cross-links.
- [Diamond Model](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/diamond) (dfir): Adversary — capability — infrastructure — victim — reference.
- [OWASP Top 10](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/owasp) (dfir): Web 2021 — API 2023 — LLM 2025 reference + checklist.
- [MITRE Matrix](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/mitre-matrix) (dfir): Static reference view of the MITRE ATT&CK matrix with tactic/technique lookup.
- [Tabletop Exercises](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/dfir/tabletop) (dfir): Scenario-driven tabletop exercises — pick a scenario, run it.

## Threat Intel platform surfaces (124+ routes)

- [Actor Directory](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/actors/directory) (threatintel): Unified actor browser — MITRE ATT&CK, MISP Galaxy, and platform DB.
- [Actor Timeline](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/actors/timeline) (threatintel): Posting activity and operational tempo per actor.
- [Actor DNA](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/actors/dna) (threatintel): TTP signatures and infrastructure fingerprints.
- [Actor Usernames](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/actors/usernames) (threatintel): Search forum handles across 2M+ records.
- [Attribution Framework](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/actors/attribution) (threatintel): Attribution framework and analysis.
- [Threat Actor Catalog](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/actors/catalog) (threatintel): Curated profiles — aliases, countries, malware, TTPs.
- [Actor Graph](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/actors/graph) (threatintel): Visualize actor → actor → IOC connections.
- [APT Tracker](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/apt-tracker) (threatintel): APT group tracker organised by region — China, Russia, Iran, North Korea, NATO, Middle East, Israel.
- [Most Wanted Actors](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/most-wanted) (threatintel): Top-priority threat actors — LockBit, Cl0p, Scattered Spider, BlackCat, and other high-impact groups.
- [Extremist Groups](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/extremists) (threatintel): Ideology-driven extremist group tracking with indicators and monitoring sources.
- [Online Predators](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/predators) (threatintel): Online predator categories, regional risk, and intervention resources.
- [Active Campaigns](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/campaigns/active) (threatintel): Active campaign tracker with status, severity, and IOC rollups.
- [Campaign Lifecycle](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/campaigns/lifecycle) (threatintel): Discovery → exploitation → actions on objectives.
- [Campaign Generator](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/campaigns/generator) (threatintel): AI-powered campaign generation for tabletop exercises.
- [Cross-Campaign](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/campaigns/cross) (threatintel): Find connections across campaigns, actors, and IOCs.
- [Daily & Weekly Briefings](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/briefings) (threatintel): Tactical digests with IOCs, severity, and detection guidance.
- [Live IOC Stream](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/iocs/live) (threatintel): Real-time IOC feed from 12+ providers — IP, domain, hash, URL.
- [IOC Enrichment](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/iocs/enrichment) (threatintel): Pivot and enrich any indicator across VT, AbuseIPDB, Shodan, OTX.
- [IOC Feeds](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/iocs/feeds) (threatintel): Structured indicator feeds ready for SIEM, EDR, or CTI ingestion.
- [Entity Resolution](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/iocs/entity) (threatintel): Resolve entities across intel sources — actor, malware, campaign.
- [C2 Tracker](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/iocs/c2) (threatintel): Live C2 infrastructure tracker — Cobalt Strike, Sliver, Mythic, 30+ families.
- [Threat Map](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/iocs/map) (threatintel): Geo-visualization of IOCs by country and ASN.
- [Cross-Correlate](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/iocs/cross) (threatintel): Cross-source IOC correlation — single-feed vs multi-feed confidence.
- [IOC Correlation](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/iocs/correlation) (threatintel): IOC correlation analysis with timeline.
- [Aggregated Feeds](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/iocs/aggregated) (threatintel): Aggregated feed browser — what each provider ships.
- [Observable DB](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/iocs/observable) (threatintel): Every indicator seen, with provenance.
- [CVE Intel](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/cves/cves) (threatintel): Unified CVE intelligence — NVD + KEV + EPSS + exploit availability.
- [GitHub Advisories](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/cves/advisories) (threatintel): GitHub security advisories with affected versions and patches.
- [CVE Resources](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/cves/resources) (threatintel): CVE resource catalogs — patch priority, exploit DB, vendor bulletins.
- [Malware IOCs](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/malware/iocs) (threatintel): Malware IOC feeds across 50+ families.
- [Malware Vault](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/malware/vault) (threatintel): Malware sample vault with hashes and metadata.
- [Malware Sandbox](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/malware/sandbox) (threatintel): Hash lookup across 10+ sandbox platforms — consensus verdict.
- [Malicious Packages](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/malware/packages) (threatintel): Malicious package tracking — npm, PyPI, RubyGems, Maven, NuGet.
- [Malpedia](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/malware/malpedia) (threatintel): Malpedia malware encyclopedia — families, YARA, references.
- [Maltrail Trails](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/malware/maltrail) (threatintel): Maltrail detection trails for known malware.
- [Feed Catalog](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/feeds/catalog) (threatintel): Feed file browser with format and sample preview.
- [Feed Sources](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/feeds/sources) (threatintel): Feed source registry with enabled/disabled state.
- [Feed Quality](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/feeds/quality) (threatintel): Feed quality metrics — freshness, accuracy, FP rate.
- [Feed Scheduler](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/feeds/scheduler) (threatintel): Feed scheduling and orchestration — cron, retry, backoff.
- [Threat Feeds](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/feeds/threatfeeds) (threatintel): Curated threat intelligence feeds from 50+ providers.
- [My Threat Intel](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/feeds/mythreatintel) (threatintel): My curated threat-intel feed — personal bookmarks and follows.
- [Source Health](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/source-health) (threatintel): Operational status, SLO metrics, and NATO Admiralty trust grades for every upstream feed.
- [Social Firehose](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/social/firehose) (threatintel): Multi-platform social media firehose.
- [Tech & AI News](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/social/news) (threatintel): Tech and AI news aggregation.
- [Crypto Scam Feed](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/social/crypto-scam) (threatintel): Crypto scam feed — wallet addresses, drainers, phishing sites.
- [Telegram Intelligence Hub](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/telegram) (threatintel): Unified Telegram CTI workspace — free cross-source search, KPIs, and entry points to all Telegram surfaces (leak monitor, IOC pipeline, channel discovery, settings).
- [Telegram Leak Monitor](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/telegram-monitor) (threatintel): Telegram Intelligence Hub — 7 tabs: firehose (merged cross-source stream), leak feed, channel search (tgstat-backed), statistics, channel discovery, linked actors (channel → MITRE pivot), and settings.
- [Telegram IOC Pipeline](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/telegram-iocs) (threatintel): Telegram-leaked IOCs flowing into the cross-source consensus — hashes, IPs, domains, CVEs, URLs from monitored channels (7-day window).
- [Dark Web Watch](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/darkweb/watch) (threatintel): Dark-web monitoring dashboard.
- [Darknet Markets Timeline](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/darkweb/markets) (threatintel): Darknet market timelines — Empire, Genesis, Hydra successors.
- [Breach Forums](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/darkweb/forums) (threatintel): Breach forum tracker — posts, threads, user activity.
- [DeepDarkCTI](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/darkweb/deepdark) (threatintel): DeepDark CTI sources — vetted onion feeds.
- [Cybercrime](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/darkweb/crime) (threatintel): Cybercrime ecosystem intelligence — actors, services, pricing.
- [Physical Bitcoin Attacks](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/darkweb/bitcoin) (threatintel): Physical Bitcoin attack tracking — wrench attacks, kidnappings.
- [Infostealer Logs](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/darkweb/infostealer) (threatintel): Infostealer log analysis — credentials, cookies, system fingerprints.
- [Secret Leaks](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/darkweb/leaks) (threatintel): Secret and credential leak monitoring across paste sites.
- [Breach Disclosures](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/darkweb/disclosures) (threatintel): Breach disclosure feed — official statements and regulatory filings.
- [Ransom Report](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/darkweb/ransom-report) (threatintel): Per-group ransomware CTI dossier — TTPs, victims, demands.
- [Ransomware Activity](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/darkweb/ransom-activity) (threatintel): Live ransomware activity feed — new victims, leak posts.
- [Ransomware Map](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/darkweb/ransom-map) (threatintel): Ransomware victim geo map — country, sector, group.
- [Ransomwhere](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/darkweb/ransomwhere) (threatintel): Crypto wallet directory tied to known ransom groups.
- [ransomware.live PRO](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/ransomware-live) (threatintel): Authenticated PRO surface — victim stats, recent cyberattacks, negotiations, and YARA packs.
- [Phish Feed](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/phishing/phish) (threatintel): Phishing feed aggregation — fresh URLs and lure analysis.
- [Phishing Wordlists](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/phishing/urls) (threatintel): Phishing hunting wordlists — brand, gift-card, sextortion, BEC.
- [Scam Watch](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/phishing/scam) (threatintel): Scam watch and monitoring — pig-butchering, romance, investment.
- [Cloud Threat Landscape](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/infra/cloud) (threatintel): Cloud threat landscape — AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes, SaaS.
- [Infrastructure Intel](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/infra/infra) (threatintel): Infrastructure intelligence — ASN, IP, certificate, hosting pivots.
- [Webamon](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/infra/webamon) (threatintel): Web asset monitoring — external footprint, exposed services, drift detection.
- [Domain Monitor](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/infra/domain) (threatintel): Domain monitoring — typosquats, lookalikes, certificate transparency.
- [Detection Rules](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/detections/detections) (threatintel): Detection rule catalog — Sigma, YARA, Suricata, KQL.
- [DISARM Framework](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/detections/disarm) (threatintel): DISARM red-team framework mapping.
- [YARA Hub](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/detections/yara) (threatintel): YARA rule hub — community and curated rules.
- [Threat Signal RSS](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/detections/signal) (threatintel): Threat-signal RSS feed with auto-classified indicators.
- [Research Index](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/research-hub/research) (threatintel): Long-form research posts and deep-dive analyses.
- [Threat Intel Reports](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/research-hub/reports) (threatintel): Original research reports with IOCs, detections, severity scoring.
- [AI Reports](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/research-hub/ai) (threatintel): AI-generated research reports from LLM analysis.
- [Write-ups](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/research-hub/writeups) (threatintel): Security write-ups and post-mortems.
- [Research Signal](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/research-hub/signal) (threatintel): Research-signal feed — what changed since last visit.
- [RedHunt Insights](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/research-hub/redhunt) (threatintel): RedHunt Labs threat-intel insights.
- [RedHunt Labs Research](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/research-hub/redhunt-labs) (threatintel): RedHunt Labs research feed.
- [Volexity Threat Intel](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/research-hub/volexity) (threatintel): Volexity threat-intelligence posts.
- [Research Post](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/research-hub/post) (threatintel): Individual research post (template page).
- [Attack Flow Library](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/research-hub/attack-flow) (threatintel): ATT&CK attack-flow library with reusable patterns.
- [Knowledge Graph](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/research-hub/knowledge) (threatintel): Knowledge graph of actors, malware, campaigns, IOCs.
- [ACH](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/research-hub/ach) (threatintel): Analysis of Competing Hypotheses.
- [Threat Intel Wiki](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/wiki/wiki) (threatintel): Long-form articles on Telegram OSINT, dark-web monitoring.
- [MITRE ATT&CK](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/wiki/mitre) (threatintel): MITRE ATT&CK matrix with technique pivots.
- [F3EAD](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/wiki/f3ead) (threatintel): F3EAD intelligence workflow framework.
- [Insider Threat Matrix](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/wiki/insider) (threatintel): Insider threat matrix and detection guidance.
- [OWASP AI Landscape](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/wiki/owasp) (threatintel): OWASP AI security landscape and LLM top-10.
- [LLM Threat Atlas](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/wiki/llm) (threatintel): MITRE ATLAS — LLM/AI threat atlas.
- [About the Platform](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/about) (threatintel): What is covered, data principles, and the analyst-first design intent behind the surface.
- [OSINT Framework](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/osint/framework) (threatintel): OSINT framework browser — 70+ tools organized by category.
- [OSINT CLI Tools](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/osint/cli) (threatintel): Curated CLI tools — username, email, domain, social, recon.
- [OSINT Country Map](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/osint/map) (threatintel): Country-based OSINT map — sources by jurisdiction.
- [Curated Toolbox](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/osint/toolbox) (threatintel): Curated security toolbox — hand-picked, vetted, well-maintained.
- [Free Cert Courses](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/osint/certs) (threatintel): Syberseeker’s start.me hub of free certification tracks — security, cloud, blue team, OSINT, GRC.
- [SecOps Tools](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/osint/secops) (threatintel): SecOps tools catalog — SIEM, EDR, SOAR, log shippers.
- [Threat Intel Copilot](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/tools/copilot) (threatintel): AI copilot — ask, pivot, summarize, draft.
- [MCP Search](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/tools/mcp) (threatintel): MCP (Model Context Protocol) tool search.
- [MISP Browser](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/tools/misp) (threatintel): MISP galaxy and event browser.
- [STIX Bundle Browser](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/tools/stix) (threatintel): Browse and download STIX 2.1 bundles for OpenCTI, MISP, etc.
- [Investigations](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/tools/investigations) (threatintel): Investigation case manager — open, closed, shared.
- [Watches](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/tools/watches) (threatintel): Watch lists — actor, indicator, keyword, and saved searches.
- [Investigation Workspaces](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/tools/workspaces) (threatintel): AEAD lifecycle workspaces — Acquire, Enrich, Assess, Deliver.
- [TG Intel Search](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/tools/tg-intel-search) (threatintel): Boolean search across Telegram messages — AND/OR/NOT, field qualifiers, IOC extraction.
- [Tactical Radar Tools](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/tools/socradar-tools) (threatintel): DDoS intelligence, FortiGate breach check, healthcare breach tracking.
- [Unified Search](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/tools/unified-search) (threatintel): Cross-source search across the entire platform.
- [Integrations & Settings](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/tools/settings) (threatintel): What integrations are wired in and what capability each one unlocks for the platform.
- [External Resources](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/external/external) (threatintel): Off-site cross-references — dashboards, OSINT directories, training labs.
- [Supply Chain Intel](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/external/supply) (threatintel): Supply chain intelligence — SolarWinds, 3CX, MOVEit, XZ Utils.
- [Awesome Lists](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/external/awesome) (threatintel): Curated awesome-security list — vetted, ranked, kept current.
- [Intel Dashboard](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/predictive/dashboard) (threatintel): Top-level intel dashboard — key stats, trending, top actors.
- [Global Pulse](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/predictive/global-pulse) (threatintel): Live 3D globe — 700+ events across 21 layers.
- [Threat Pulse](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/predictive/threat-pulse) (threatintel): Threat-pulse tracking — actor activity, campaign spikes, geo shifts.
- [CertStream](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/predictive/certstream) (threatintel): Certificate transparency live feed.
- [PIR Dashboard](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/predictive/pir) (threatintel): Priority Intelligence Requirements dashboard.
- [Metrics](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/predictive/metrics) (threatintel): Ten-panel metrics board.
- [Analytics & Ops](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/predictive/analytics) (threatintel): Platform health, feed reliability, and intel metrics.
- [Predictions](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/predictive/predictions) (threatintel): Forward-looking threat predictions with confidence.
- [Predictive Intel](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/predictive/predictive) (threatintel): AI-driven threat forecasting from current trends.
- [Analyze](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/predictive/analyze) (threatintel): Intelligence analysis workspace.
- [Assessments](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/predictive/assessments) (threatintel): Security assessments and risk scoring.
- [Observe](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/predictive/observe) (threatintel): Observation dashboard — what is happening right now.
- [SOC Dashboard](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/soc-dashboard) (threatintel): Unified tactical SOC view — ransomware, vulnerabilities, and IOC stream panels.
- [Live Center — Web OSINT](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/threatintel/live-center) (threatintel): Browser-based live OSINT tools with install, example, and reference URL per tool.

## MCP server (AI agent tools, 98 tools)

Endpoint: https://pranithjain.qzz.io/api/mcp (streamable HTTP)
Auth: Authorization: Bearer <api-key> or X-API-Key: <key>
Manifest: https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp-manifest.json
Drop-in configs: https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp/{claude-desktop,cursor,vscode-mcp}.json

- [analyze_phishing_email](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:phishing): Analyze raw email source for phishing indicators. Parses headers, checks SPF/DKIM/DMARC, extracts URLs, and computes a risk score with flags.
- [analyze_phishing_url](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:phishing): Analyze a URL for phishing indicators. Checks against PhishTank, OpenPhish, URLhaus, and performs visual similarity analysis.
- [analyze_report](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:analysis): Unified per-report analyzer. Runs summary + IOC extraction (with allowlist + confidence) + MITRE ATT&CK TTP mapping + 5W context + CVE extraction + image-OCR + STIX 2.1 bundle in a single round-trip. Accepts text, URL, or both; optionally takes image URLs to OCR.
- [check_breach](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:breach): Check if an email address or domain has been exposed in known data breaches. Returns breach names, dates, and exposed data types.
- [check_ioc](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:ioc): Check reputation of an IP address, domain, URL, or file hash (MD5/SHA1/SHA256) across 30+ threat intelligence providers. Returns composite score, admiralty grade, and per-provider verdicts.
- [correlate_iocs](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:ioc): Search correlated IOCs. Find relationships between indicators — shared infrastructure, overlapping campaigns, and linked threat actors.
- [cve_health](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:cve): Check the health of CVE data pipelines. Validates NVD API, EPSS API, CISA KEV, GitHub API rate limit, KV intel cache (EPSS coverage, KEV count, field completeness), and Exploit-DB mirror availability. Returns overall status (healthy/degraded/unhealthy) with per-check details.
- [cve_poc_map](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:cve): Get the cached CVE-to-GitHub-repo mapping. Pass ?id=CVE-XXXX-XXXXX for a single CVE, or ?year=YYYY for a year-scoped index of all mapped CVEs. Results are KV-cached for 24h.
- [cyber_news](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Aggregate cybersecurity news from 11 RSS feeds across 5 tiers (Advisory, Exploit, Research, Vendor, Community). Supports tier filtering and keyword search. Sources: CISA, Rapid7, Packet Storm, BleepingComputer, Hacker News, GitHub Security, ZDI, Reddit netsec/exploitdev/bugbounty.
- [enrich_actor](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:actor): Get a threat actor profile. Returns aliases, country attribution, MITRE ATT&CK techniques, known campaigns, and associated malware families.
- [extract_fivew](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:analysis): Extract the classic 5W grid (who/what/when/where/why) from a free-text report. Single LLM call; returns structured JSON with a per-grid confidence score.
- [extract_iocs_from_image](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:analysis): Fetch an image and run Workers AI vision over it to extract IOCs that are only visible in screenshots (IPs, domains, URLs, hashes, CVEs, emails). Returns the OCR text + the per-IOC confidence band.
- [extract_ttps](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:analysis): Extract MITRE ATT&CK techniques from a free-text threat report. Returns technique IDs, tactic labels, confidence (high/medium/low), and the supporting evidence string. Combines a deterministic keyword scanner with an LLM pass and merges the results.
- [generate_yara_rule](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:detection): Generate a YARA detection rule using AI. Provide a description of what to detect, and optionally known strings, malware family name, and target file type. Returns a syntactically valid YARA rule with metadata.
- [get_blocklists](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:intel): Get pre-generated firewall blocklists in pfSense, iptables, and Suricata formats. Derived from aggregated threat intel feeds.
- [get_cert_in_advisories](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) advisories — vendor-reported vulnerabilities affecting Indian enterprises, with severity, CVEs, products affected, and the official CIAD-YYYY-NNNN ID. Filter by CVE, year, severity, or keyword.
- [get_cross_report_graph](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Cross-report knowledge-graph snapshot. Returns the top N most-referenced nodes (IOCs, actors, malware, CVEs, techniques, campaigns) across every ingested source, with the edges that connect them. Filter by node type and time window.
- [get_detections](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Get the latest detection rules feed — Sigma, YARA, and Snort rules mapped to threat actors, malware families, and MITRE ATT&CK techniques.
- [get_domain_certs](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:domain): Get recent certificates for a domain from Certificate Transparency logs. Shows new subdomains, certificate details, and any alerts.
- [get_domain_history](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:domain): Get the WHOIS history for a domain. Returns all historical registration snapshots, ownership changes, registrar changes, and nameserver changes over time. Essential for tracking domain ownership transfers and identifying infrastructure reuse by threat actors.
- [get_feed_status](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Get the health and freshness status of all 30+ threat intelligence feed sources. Shows last update time, error rates, and data volume.
- [get_ioc_lifecycle](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Get the lifecycle data for an IOC — when it first appeared, last seen, activity trend, and decay rate. Use this to understand if an indicator is still active or dormant.
- [get_live_iocs](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:intel): Get the latest live IOC feed — real-time indicators of compromise aggregated from 20+ sources including blocklists, tweet feeds, abuse.ch, and community submissions.
- [get_ransomware_activity](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:intel): Get recent ransomware activity — latest victims, group activity, and leak-site posts from ransomware.live and other trackers.
- [get_relationships](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:intel): Get the relationship graph for an IOC — shows connections to threat actors, malware families, campaigns, CVEs, and other indicators.
- [get_supply_chain_attacks](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:intel): Software supply-chain compromise incidents (npm/PyPI/container/AI-agent ecosystems) from supplychainattack.org — title, status, severity, ecosystems, attack vectors, blast radius, remediation, package IOCs, and GHSA sources. Filter by ecosystem/status/severity.
- [get_threat_pulse](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Get a global threat overview — top active threat actors, trending malware families, most exploited CVEs, and geopolitical cyber events from the past week.
- [get_today_briefing](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:intel): Get today's threat intelligence briefing. A curated digest of the latest CVEs, ransomware activity, data breaches, and emerging threats from the past 24 hours.
- [get_trending_iocs](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Get the most active IOCs in the last 24 hours. Returns indicators with highest observation counts and scores, useful for identifying emerging threats.
- [google_dorks](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:osint): Generate and execute Google dork queries for a domain. Useful for finding exposed files, login pages, and sensitive information.
- [hr_account](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:hudson): Check Hudson Rock Cavalier API account status, permissions, and quota. Use to verify the API key is valid.
- [hr_assets_discovery](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:hudson): Discover all compromised URLs for a domain (attack surface mapping). Returns URLs where credentials were stolen, occurrence counts, and compromise types.
- [hr_domain_overview](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:hudson): Get domain compromise overview statistics from Hudson Rock — compromised employee/user counts, last compromise dates, and upload timelines. Useful for risk posture assessment.
- [hr_infection_analysis](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:hudson): AI-powered infection source analysis for a specific stealer log. Returns the likely infection URL, confidence score, timeline of suspicious activity, and analyst summary. Works best with Lumma stealers.
- [hr_search_domain](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:hudson): Search for domain-wide infostealer compromises via Hudson Rock Cavalier API. Returns compromised employees, users, and third-party exposures with stealer families and infection dates.
- [hr_search_email](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:hudson): Search for compromised credentials by email address via Hudson Rock Cavalier API. Returns infostealer infections, stealer families, compromised URLs, and credential types (employee/user/third-party).
- [hr_search_ip](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:hudson): Search for compromises by IP address or CIDR range via Hudson Rock Cavalier API. Useful for IR when you have a suspicious IP.
- [hr_search_username](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:hudson): Search for compromised credentials by username via Hudson Rock Cavalier API.
- [hr_third_party_risk](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:hudson): Assess third-party / supply-chain risk for a domain. Returns employee URLs, third-party service URLs, and user URLs where credentials were compromised — indicating supply chain exposure.
- [ioc_watchlist_add](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:ioc): Add an IOC to the watchlist for proactive alerting. Supported types: ip, domain, url, hash, cve, email. Alerts fire when the IOC appears in feeds.
- [ioc_watchlist_alerts](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:ioc): List recent alerts from the IOC watchlist.
- [ioc_watchlist_list](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:ioc): List all watched IOCs. Optionally filter by type.
- [ioc_watchlist_stats](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:ioc): Get watchlist dashboard stats: total watches, alerts by type, webhook delivery rate.
- [list_briefings](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:intel): List recent threat intelligence briefings (daily and weekly). Returns slug, date, type, and summary for each.
- [lookup_asn](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:domain): ASN intelligence lookup. Returns AS name, country, network ranges, RIR registration, and BGP peer info.
- [lookup_cve](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:cve): Look up a CVE by ID. Returns description, CVSS score, EPSS probability, CISA KEV status, affected products, and references.
- [lookup_domain](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:domain): Domain intelligence lookup. Returns DNS records (A, AAAA, MX, NS, TXT, SOA), WHOIS/RDAP registration data, CT log (certificate transparency) entries, SPF/DKIM/DMARC email authentication analysis, and threat intel hits from blocklists and IOC feeds.
- [lookup_ip_geo](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:domain): Get IP geolocation, ASN, company, and privacy detection (VPN/proxy/tor/hosting). Uses IPinfo and Spur.us for anonymization detection.
- [lookup_mitre](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Look up a MITRE ATT&CK technique by ID. Returns technique name, description, tactics, mitigations, and detection guidance.
- [notebook_add_entry](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:notebook): Add a note, IOC, finding, timeline event, or artifact to a notebook.
- [notebook_create](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:notebook): Create a new investigation notebook.
- [notebook_delete](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:notebook): Delete a notebook and all its entries.
- [notebook_get](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:notebook): Get a notebook with all its entries.
- [notebook_list](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:notebook): List investigation notebooks. Each notebook is a persistent investigation session with notes, IOCs, findings, and timeline entries stored in D1.
- [notebook_update](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:notebook): Update a notebook title, description, status, or severity.
- [parse_threat_report](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:analysis): Parse a threat intelligence report or article to extract structured data: IOCs (IPs, domains, URLs, hashes), threat actors, malware families, MITRE ATT&CK techniques, CVEs, targeted sectors, and an executive summary. Use this when analyzing threat reports, blog posts, or incident write-ups.
- [passive_dns_overlap](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:pdns): Find IPs shared between multiple domains (infrastructure overlap detection). Useful for mapping shared malicious hosting.
- [passive_dns_query](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:pdns): Query passive DNS for a domain or IP. Returns historical DNS resolutions, infrastructure migrations, and fast-flux detection. Sources: VirusTotal, URLscan, crt.sh, CIRCL.
- [passive_dns_reverse](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:pdns): Reverse passive DNS lookup: find all domains that historically resolved to a given IP. Reads from accumulated D1 cache.
- [pivot_domain](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:domain): Pivot across domains by shared registrant attributes. Find other domains owned by the same entity by matching registrant email, organization, nameservers, or registrar. Critical for mapping attacker infrastructure — if a malicious domain shares its registrant email with 50 other domains, those are likely all owned by the same threat actor.
- [poc_scan](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Search GitHub for public exploit/PoC repositories for a CVE. Returns repo URLs, star counts, language, age, and whether the repo has actual code. Bypasses GitHub 1000-result limit via monthly pagination.
- [scan_website](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:exposure): Scan a website for security issues — checks security headers, SSL certificate, technologies, and potential vulnerabilities.
- [search_malpedia](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:search): Search Malpedia for malware families or threat actors. Returns matching entries with descriptions and references.
- [search_malware](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:search): Search for malware families. Returns family info, YARA rules, samples, and references from Malpedia.
- [search_registrant](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:domain): Search for all domains registered by a specific email address or organization name. Returns domains, registration dates, and snapshot counts. Useful for finding all infrastructure operated by a known threat actor.
- [search_triage](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:search): Search Recorded Future Triage sandbox for malware samples by family, tag, hash, URL, or domain. Returns analysis results, behavioral reports, and extracted configs.
- [si_enrich_ip](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:si): Enrich a single IPv4/IPv6 address using the platform's IPinfo / AbuseIPDB / Shodan / Shodan-InternetDB / VPNAPI providers. Returns the same shape as upstream security-investigator/enrich_ips.py. Use si_enrich_ip_batch for up to 25 IPs in one call.
- [si_enrich_ip_batch](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:si): Enrich up to 25 IP addresses in one call. Returns an array of the same shape as si_enrich_ip. Order is preserved. IPs that fail validation are returned with a single "validator:failed" diagnostic and empty enrichment fields.
- [si_get_automation](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:si): Return a scheduled-workflow definition (Copilot App / GitHub Actions) for running the skills unattended. Three automations ship: daily-threat-pulse, daily-mcp-auth-health-check, weekly-threat-intel-campaign.
- [si_get_doc](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:si): Return the full markdown body of a single knowledge-base doc. Get slugs from si_list_docs.
- [si_get_query](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:si): Return the full markdown body of a single KQL query (Defender XDR / Sentinel hunting query, IoC correlation, or campaign playbook). Use si_list_queries first to discover slugs.
- [si_get_ref](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:si): Return a reference dataset by name. Get names from si_list_ref. Common: mitre-attck-enterprise (MITRE ATT&CK enterprise matrix, ~32 KB), known-kql-tables (M365 Defender table inventory, ~17 KB), m365-platform-coverage (coverage map, ~16 KB), ingestion-qN (Sentinel ingestion-scan query result schemas).
- [si_get_routing_prompt](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:si): Return the upstream .github/copilot-instructions.md verbatim — the universal skill-detection / routing prompt. Clients should load this once at session start to learn how to map natural language to the right si_* tool. ~91 KB.
- [si_get_script](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:si): Return the raw body of a PowerShell script or detection-manifest. Use si_list_scripts to discover filenames. The PowerShell scripts target Microsoft Defender XDR / Sentinel / M365 — they are NOT executable in the Worker; copy them to a PowerShell 7+ session locally to run.
- [si_get_skill](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:si): Return the full SKILL.md body (markdown) for a single security investigation skill. Use si_list_skills first to discover slugs.
- [si_hypos_generate](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:si): HYPOS: hypothesis engine for threat hunting. Given a free-text anomaly description and optional IOCs / environment, return ranked hypotheses with kill-chain phase, MITRE techniques, what-to-look-for signals, sample KQL, and matched SI skills.
- [si_kql_to_ah_url](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:si): Encode a KQL query into a Defender XDR Advanced Hunting deep link. Mirrors upstream kql_to_ah_url.py: UTF-16LE → GZip → Base64url. Optionally append &tid=<tenant_id> for cross-tenant linking. Returns the URL.
- [si_list_docs](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:si): List the 10 deep-dive knowledge-base docs from the upstream repo (Sentinel Exposure Graph guide, signinlog anomalies KQL cookbook, identity protection, honeypot investigation, ingestion cost best practices, etc). Each is a long-form markdown guide.
- [si_list_queries](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:si): List the KQL queries shipped in this Worker (Defender XDR / Sentinel hunt library replicated from SCStelz/security-investigator, MIT). Filter by domain (cloud / email / endpoint / identity / incidents / network / threat-intelligence) or free-text keyword.
- [si_list_ref](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:si): List the reference datasets available via si_get_ref: MITRE ATT&CK enterprise catalog, known KQL tables for the M365 platform, M365 platform coverage matrix, and the 11 Sentinel ingestion-scan query schemas.
- [si_list_scripts](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:si): List the 5 PowerShell / detection-manifest assets that ship in the SI bundle: Deploy-CustomDetections.ps1 (batch-deploy Defender XDR rules), Invoke-MitreScan.ps1 (full MITRE coverage scanner), Invoke-IngestionScan.ps1 (Sentinel ingestion health), example-detection-manifest.json (input template), sentinel-ingestion-drilldown.md (companion guide).
- [si_list_skills](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:si): List the security investigation skills shipped in this Worker (replicated from SCStelz/security-investigator, MIT). Each skill is a guided KQL+playbook workflow. Filter by category or free-text keyword.
- [si_parse_email_headers](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:si): MAILSCOPE: parse raw email headers, extract the Received hop chain, compute SPF/DKIM/DMARC verdicts, and flag spoofing/impersonation patterns. Returns a 0-100 risk score.
- [si_parse_text](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:si): PARSE-X: extract IOCs, file paths, registry keys, processes, DLLs, CVEs, MITRE techniques, hashes, emails, ports, MACs, and ASNs from raw text. Handles defang (hxxp, [.], (dot)) and Cyrillic/Greek homographs.
- [si_promptvault_categories](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:si): PROMPTVAULT: list the valid prompt categories.
- [si_promptvault_create](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:si): PROMPTVAULT: add a new prompt to the vault. Returns the created entry.
- [si_promptvault_get](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:si): PROMPTVAULT: fetch a single prompt by slug. Auto-increments the download counter.
- [si_promptvault_list](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:si): PROMPTVAULT: list community AI prompts for SOC analysts, detection engineers, and threat hunters. Filter by category, tag, or text search.
- [si_promptvault_rate](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:si): PROMPTVAULT: rate a prompt 1-5 stars. Returns the updated entry with new rating count and average.
- [si_render_png](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:si): Render an SVG dashboard and rasterise it to PNG (base64-encoded in the JSON response). Same manifest + data shape as si_render_svg, but the output is a portable bitmap you can embed in markdown, email, or social previews. Uses the bundled @resvg/resvg-wasm + Hanken Grotesk TTF.
- [si_render_svg](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:si): Render an SVG dashboard from a manifest + data. Returns a self-contained <svg> string with inline styles, no external dependencies. Use si_render_svg_dashboard(slug) to get the canonical manifest for a skill, then pass its body as manifestYaml here. Supports all 14 widget types: title-banner, kpi-card, delta-kpi-card, score-card, donut-chart, stacked-bar-chart, horizontal-bar-chart, line-chart, waterfall-chart, sparkline, progress-bar, table-widget, recommendation-cards, assessment-banner, coverage-matrix. Unknown types render as a dashed warning panel.
- [si_render_svg_dashboard](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:si): Return the SVG widget manifest (YAML) for a skill that ships one (14 of 25 skills do). The manifest declares canvas, palette, and a list of widget instances to render. Pair with si_get_skill({slug: "svg-dashboard"}) for the component-library reference. Returns {hasManifest:false,...} if the skill has no SVG manifest.
- [si_shiftlog_close](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:si): SHIFTLOG: close a shift entry (sets ended_at to now, or to a provided ISO timestamp).
- [si_shiftlog_create](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:si): SHIFTLOG: start a new SOC shift handover entry. Returns the created entry including its id (sl_...).
- [si_shiftlog_get](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:si): SHIFTLOG: fetch a single shift handover entry by id (sl_...).
- [si_shiftlog_list](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:si): SHIFTLOG: list recent shift handover entries. Filter by author, shift, or openOnly (excludes closed shifts).
- [si_shiftlog_update](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:si): SHIFTLOG: patch a shift entry (notes, open cases, IOCs, escalations, endedAt).
- [si_stats](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:si): Return cache + manifest stats for the Security Investigator data: index loaded, body-cache sizes and hit ratios. Useful for diagnosing cold-start latency.
- [soc_cve_report](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Generate a SOC CVE intelligence report. Takes a list of up to 50 CVE IDs and bundles CVE lookup + PoC scan + health check into a downloadable CSV or Markdown report. Returns executive summary, CVSS/EPSS/KEV details, PoC repos, and pipeline health.
- [tg_boolean_search](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Search Telegram leak messages with boolean AND/OR/NOT operators and field qualifiers. Fields: text, channel.title, channel.username, severity, leak_type. Supports wildcards (prefix*) and exact phrases ("quoted").
- [tg_saved_search_create](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Save a Telegram boolean search query for one-click reuse.
- [tg_saved_search_delete](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Delete a saved Telegram search query.
- [tg_saved_searches_list](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): List saved Telegram boolean search queries.
- [tg_timeline](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Get Telegram message volume timeline data (messages per day) with severity breakdown. Useful for visualizing activity spikes.
- [trace_crypto_address](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Trace a cryptocurrency wallet address. Returns balance, transaction history, and associated entities from blockchain explorers.
- [unified_search](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:search): Cross-source search across all threat intelligence feeds. Search by keyword, IOC, actor name, malware family, or CVE to find matching entries across briefings, live feeds, ransomware data, and more.
- [validate_yara_rule](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:detection): Validate a YARA rule syntax. Checks for balanced braces, required sections, and proper string definitions.
- [watch_domain_ct](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:domain): Add a domain to Certificate Transparency monitoring. Alerts on new subdomains, suspicious patterns, wildcard certs, and more. Uses crt.sh for unlimited free CT log queries.
- [wayback_lookup](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:domain): Check the Wayback Machine (archive.org) for historical snapshots of a URL. Useful for tracking website changes or recovering deleted content.
- [ws_add_connection](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Define a relationship between two subjects in a workspace.
- [ws_add_finding](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Log a finding with source, trust score, and confidence in a workspace.
- [ws_add_subject](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Register a subject (entity) in a workspace investigation.
- [ws_create](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Create a new investigation workspace for AEAD lifecycle tracking.
- [ws_export_stix](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Export workspace indicators as STIX 2.1 bundle or flat IOC list.
- [ws_exposure](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Calculate composite exposure score (0-100) for a target based on IOC reputation, breach exposure, infrastructure, attack surface, and threat intel.
- [ws_get](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Get a workspace with all subjects, connections, findings, and timeline.
- [ws_list](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): List investigation workspaces. Each workspace is a full AEAD-lifecycle case with subjects, connections, findings, and timeline.
- [ws_render_graph](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Render an ASCII box-drawing relationship graph, timeline, or risk heatmap from workspace data.
- [ws_workflow_advance](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Advance a workspace to the next AEAD phase (Acquire→Enrich→Assess→Deliver→Complete).
- [ws_workflow_summary](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Get workspace summary: phase progress, findings breakdown, recommended commands.

## REST API (OpenAPI 3.1)

Spec: https://pranithjain.qzz.io/api/v1/openapi.json
Browser: https://pranithjain.qzz.io/api/docs (Scalar)
Health: https://pranithjain.qzz.io/api/v1/health
Feed status: https://pranithjain.qzz.io/api/v1/feed-status

## Open-source packages published from this codebase

- cti-text-extract - synchronous, dependency-free CTI entity extractor.
- stix21-builder - STIX 2.1 bundle builder with deterministic UUIDv5 IDs.
- cti-ioc-enrich - pluggable IOC enrichment framework.
- telegram-preview-parser - parse Telegram channel previews to structured JSON.
- deepdarkcti-parser - parse the fastfire/deepdarkCTI markdown index to typed JSON.
- cti-platform - the threat-intel platform as a standalone app.
- DFIR-PLATFORM - the DFIR toolkit as a standalone app.
- cti-stix-connector (Python) - ingests CSV/JSON/MyThreatIntel data into valid STIX 2.1.

## Live status

Per-feed freshness: https://pranithjain.qzz.io/status
Hourly cron-driven refresh; all caches have a 5-15 minute TTL.

---
Generated at build time. Pull /mcp-manifest.json for the authoritative tool list and 
/api/v1/openapi.json for the authoritative API spec.
